title: SuperBEST v4 Node Cost Analysis: Quantum Mechanics Equations
key_insight: DEML(x,1) = exp(-x) costs 1n. The Boltzmann factor exp(-E/kT) becomes 1n after the division, making statistical mechanics structurally cheap. Fermi-Dirac and Bose-Einstein are EML-native.

equations.name: Boltzmann factor

cost_breakdown.note: compute kT denominator; skip if kT is a constant

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Beer-Lambert attenuation: I = I_0 * exp(-alpha*x) — same exp(-x) structure, 5n
Radioactive decay: N(t) = N_0 * exp(-lambda*t) — identical 5n pattern
RC discharge: V(t) = V_0 * exp(-t/RC) — same form, 5n
All are DEML(x,1) after one division
equations.notes: The core primitive of statistical mechanics. With kT as a precomputed constant it reduces to 3n (div + DEML).

equations.name: Fermi-Dirac distribution

cost_breakdown.note: EML primitive; positive or negative argument

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Sigmoid / logistic function: sigma(x) = 1/(1+exp(-x)) — structurally identical, same 9n (with sign flip absorbed into sub)
ELO expected score: E_A = 1/(1+10^(delta/400)) — same form with base-10 exp, ~9n
Logistic regression output: p = 1/(1+exp(-w·x)) — same 9n after dot product
Softmax binary case: exp(x_1)/(exp(x_1)+exp(x_2)) reduces to Fermi-Dirac form
equations.notes: The Fermi-Dirac distribution IS the physics sigmoid. The cross-domain isomorphism connects quantum statistics, neural networks, and probabilistic classifiers at the node-cost level.

equations.name: Bose-Einstein distribution

cost_breakdown.note: exp((E-mu)/kT) > 1 for E > mu, so result is positive

cross_domain_isomorphisms: No standard sigmoid isomorphism; the -1 (vs +1 in FD) is physically fundamental — marks the difference between fermion exclusion and boson bunching
Planck distribution prefactor uses this as its core kernel
Structure: recip(sub(exp(x), 1)) — a novel EML primitive pattern not found in classical ML
equations.notes: One node cheaper than Fermi-Dirac (8n vs 9n) because sub costs 2n vs add_pos 3n. The sub(exp,1) pattern is the boson signature.

equations.name: Planck spectral radiance

cost_breakdown.subtotal: 8n (BE kernel) + 4n (h*nu/kT) = 12n total for photon BE factor

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Planck = prefactor * Bose-Einstein(photons, mu=0)
The 1/(exp(x)-1) kernel appears in Riemann zeta function evaluations via Mellin transform
Stefan-Boltzmann law emerges by integrating Planck over all frequencies — the T^4 law is a consequence of the BE kernel
equations.notes: Cost dominated by the frequency prefactor. The BE kernel itself is 8n; the photon energy computation adds 4n more.

equations.name: Hydrogen atom energy levels

cost_breakdown.note: n^2 where n is principal quantum number
13.6 eV is the Rydberg constant in eV, treated as constant

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Balmer series wavelengths: lambda = const / (1/n_f^2 - 1/n_i^2) — same pow+recip pattern
Kepler orbit energy: E = -G*M*m/(2a) — same recip structure for bound states
Rydberg formula generalizes to all one-electron atoms: E_n = -Z^2 * 13.6 eV / n^2
equations.notes: Clean 7n computation. The negation (2n) reflects the physics: bound states must have E < 0.

equations.name: de Broglie wavelength

cost_breakdown.note: momentum p = mv; skip if momentum is the input
h is Planck's constant, a precomputed constant

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Any wavelength = speed/frequency formula: lambda = v/f — same 2n div pattern
Compton wavelength: lambda_C = h/(m*c) — same structure, c replaces v
Thermal de Broglie: lambda_th = h/sqrt(2*pi*m*kT) — adds sqrt+mul steps
equations.notes: Minimal cost; essentially a single division once momentum is known.

equations.name: Heisenberg uncertainty principle

cost_breakdown.note: compute the product to check against constant hbar/2
pure constant — no computation needed at runtime

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Bandwidth-time uncertainty in signal processing: Delta_t * Delta_f >= 1/(4*pi) — identical inequality structure
Entropic uncertainty relations in information theory
Fisher information / Cramer-Rao bound: Var(theta) >= 1/I(theta) — same product-vs-constant pattern
equations.notes: This is an inequality, not a function evaluation. Runtime cost is just the product (2n) to compare against a known constant.

equations.name: Particle-in-a-box wavefunction
cross_domain_isomorphisms: Fourier sine series basis functions: same n*pi*x/L argument structure
Standing waves on a string: y_n(x,t) = A*sin(n*pi*x/L)*cos(omega_n*t) — same spatial factor
Acoustic resonances in pipes: same boundary condition math
equations.notes: 13n total. If L is fixed, sqrt(2/L) becomes a constant reducing to 9n (skip div+sqrt, keep only mul(const, sin)).

equations.name: Particle-in-a-box energy levels

cost_breakdown.note: 2*m*L^2 treated as a single physical constant

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Same n^2 scaling as hydrogen: E_n ~ 1/n^2 (H atom) vs E_n ~ n^2 (box) — inverse relationship reflects free vs confined
Rotational energy levels of a rigid rotor: E_J = J*(J+1)*hbar^2/(2I) — similar structure with pow
equations.notes: Identical 7n cost to hydrogen energy levels. Both are quadratic-in-n patterns dominated by pow(n,2)=3n.

equations.name: Canonical partition function

cost_breakdown.note: N-1 additions; all Boltzmann factors are positive

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Softmax denominator in neural networks: sum(exp(x_i)) — same structure, same cost formula
Normalizing constant in Boltzmann policy (RL): pi(a) = exp(Q(a)) / Z
Evidence in Bayesian inference: p(D) = sum_i p(D|theta_i)*p(theta_i) — same sum-of-exponentials pattern
All are 6N-3 for N options when using EML primitives
equations.notes: Linear in N. The softmax denominator and partition function are the same computation — a deep ML/physics connection.

equations.name: Maxwell-Boltzmann speed distribution

steps.note: T^(-3/2); absorb m/(2*pi*k) as constant multiplier

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Maxwell-Boltzmann is a chi distribution (k=3 degrees of freedom) scaled
Gaussian velocity distribution: each component v_x ~ exp(-m*v_x^2/2kT) — 5n per component, 3D product = 17n
Log-normal distribution has similar x^2 * exp(-x^2) envelope structure
Rayleigh distribution (2D): f(v) ~ v * exp(-v^2/(2*sigma^2)) — same pattern minus one pow dimension
equations.notes: 19n total. The v^2 can be shared between the polynomial prefactor and the Boltzmann exponent, saving one pow(v,2)=3n call.

summary_table.name: Particle-in-box energy
summary_table.isomorphism: rigid rotor E_J; inverse of H-atom n^2 scaling

global_observations: The Boltzmann factor exp(-E/kT) = DEML(E/kT,1) is the fundamental 1n primitive after one division; it propagates cheapness across all of statistical mechanics.
Fermi-Dirac (9n) = sigmoid: the most important cross-domain isomorphism discovered. Quantum statistics and deep learning share identical node structure.
Bose-Einstein (8n) is one node cheaper than Fermi-Dirac due to sub(exp,1) costing less than add_pos(1,exp). The -1 vs +1 is a 1-node physical distinction.
Both particle-in-box (7n) and hydrogen (7n) energy levels cost identically, despite one scaling as n^2 and the other as 1/n^2.
Partition function cost is exactly 6N-3, matching softmax denominator — statistical mechanics and attention mechanisms are the same computation.
Maxwell-Boltzmann and Planck both reach 19n; they are the most expensive in this set due to polynomial-times-exponential structure.
